avoid: Do not promote a shape-agnostic implementation that regresses at d=4096.
Do not spend effort on a custom full GEMM for W @ X; W @ X dominates and should remain cuBLAS.
Do not add TORCH_CHECK validation, dtype checks, contiguity conversions, explicit d range checks, or defensive copies on the trusted-input benchmark path.
Do not continue block-size or float4 tuning of the handwritten rank-16 update before validating the simpler pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM baseline.
Do not use result caching unless the benchmark explicitly defines repeated identical inputs as the target workload.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested d values with max_abs_err 0.00146484375 and rel_l2_err 7.9e-7, so the candidate is numerically acceptable.
Aggregate speedup is only 1.004x, effectively neutral versus the torch baseline.
Performance is inconsistent by shape: d=3584 improves by 1.043x and d=4608 improves by 1.036x, but d=4096 regresses to 0.951x.
The candidate uses a cublas_plus_custom_rank16_update strategy; the profile points to rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth, suggesting the handwritten rank-16 update is not consistently beneficial.
Static warnings about no dtype checks, no TORCH_CHECK validation, and no contiguity copies are acceptable for the desired trusted-input fast path, not reasons to reject.
Compile succeeds but compile time is high at 228 seconds, which is not a runtime bottleneck but adds iteration cost.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Do not promote this candidate as a general implementation because the aggregate gain is negligible and d=4096 regresses significantly.
Move toward the minimal trusted-input pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation based on /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu instead of continuing to tune the handwritten rank-16 update.
Keep the main W @ X term as a direct cuBLAS SGEMM; do not attempt a custom full d x d GEMM.

Replace the custom rank-16 path with the cuBLAS low-rank sequence: first compute Y = W @ X, then compute U with shape {d, 16}, then accumulate A @ U.T or the equivalent row-major cuBLAS mapping into Y using beta = 1.

Use the exact 3-SGEMM mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, U shape {d,16}, ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1.

Ensure the implementation avoids explicit B.T materialization, avoids input copies, avoids explicit d range checks, uses CUDAGuard plus getCurrentCUDABlasHandle, and passes tensor.data_ptr<float>() directly inside cublasSgemm.
If this custom candidate is kept for experimentation, use shape-aware dispatch only after validating that it consistently helps d=3584 and d=4608 while falling back for d=4096; otherwise reject it.
